classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

title: The Holy Spirit Twice Given.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: For the glorification of our Lord among men is His resurrection from the dead and His ascension to heaven.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: For it is written in the Gospel according to John: "The Holy Ghost was not yet given, because that Jesus was not yet glorified."

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Now if the reason why He was not given was that Jesus was not yet glorified, He was given immediately on the glorification of Jesus.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: And since that glorification was twofold, as regards man and as regards God, twice also was the Holy Spirit given: once, when, after His resurrection from the dead, He breathed on the face of His disciples, saying, "Receive ye the Holy Ghost;" and again, ten days after His ascension to heaven.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: This number ten signifies perfection; for to the number seven which embraces all created things, is added the trinity of the Creator.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: On these things there is much pious and sober discourse among spiritual men.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: But I must keep to my point; for my business at present is not to teach you, which you might think presumptuous, but to take the part of an inquirer, and learn from you, as I tried to do for nine years without success.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Now, therefore, I have a document to believe on the subject of the Holy Spirit’s advent; and if you bid me not to believe this document, as your usual advice is not to believe ignorantly, without consideration, much less will I believe your documents.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Away, then, with all books, and disclose the truth with logical clearness, so as to leave no doubt in my mind; or bring forward books where I shall find not an imperious demand for my belief, but a trustworthy statement of what I may learn.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Perhaps you say this epistle is also of this character.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Let me, then, no longer stop at the threshold: let us see the contents.
